University Mississippi (colloquially known Miss) public research university Oxford, Mississippi. Including University Mississippi Medical Center Jackson, state's largest university enrollment. university chartered Mississippi Legislature February 1844, four years later admitted first enrollment students. university classified "R1: Doctoral High Research Activity" Carnegie Institutions Higher Education annual research development budget $121.6 million. university ranked 2018 edition News Rankings Best National Universities.

Miss center activity during American civil rights movement when race riot erupted 1962 following attempted admission James Meredith, segregated campus. While university successfully integrated that year, Confederate symbols motifs remained controversial aspect school's identity culture. response university attempted take proactive measures rebrand image, including effectively banning display Confederate flags Stadium 1997, officially abandoning Colonel mascot 2003, removing "Dixie" from Pride South marching band's repertoire 2016. 2018, following racially charged rant social media alum academic building namesake, former Chancellor Jeffrey Vitter reaffirmed university's commitment "honest open dialogue about history", making campuses "more welcoming inclusive".
"Dead House" built before Civil used morgue during war. Bodies were carried from Dead House across campus Civil Cemetery, which contains more than graves. building demolished 1958, Farley Hall--which contains Meek School Journalism Media--was erected place.
Mississippi Legislature chartered University Mississippi February 1844. university opened doors first class students four years later 1848. years, university Mississippi's only public institution higher learning, years state's only comprehensive university. Politician Pryor founding trustee.
When university opened, campus consisted buildings: dormitories, faculty houses, steward's hall, Lyceum center. Constructed from 1846 1848, Lyceum oldest building campus. Originally, Lyceum housed classrooms faculty offices university. building's north south wings were added 1903, Class 1927 donated clock above eastern portico. Lyceum home university's offices. columned facade Lyceum represented official crest university, along with date establishment.
1854, university established fourth public school United States, also began offering engineering education.
With outbreak Civil 1861, classes were interrupted when almost entire student body (135 students) from University Mississippi enlisted Confederate army, forming infantry group nicknamed University Greys. However, students were killed during war, 100% casualty rate. Most died during battles Gettysburg Vicksburg.
Lyceum used hospital during Civil both Union Confederate soldiers, especially those were wounded battle Shiloh. hundred-fifty soldiers died campus hospital were buried cemetery grounds university.
During post-war period, university former Confederate general A.P. Stewart, Rogersville, Tennessee native. served Chancellor from 1874 1886.
university became coeducational 1882 first such institution Southeast hire female faculty member, Sarah McGehee Isom, doing 1885.
student yearbook published first time 1897. contest held solicit suggestions yearbook title from student body. Elma Meek, student, submitted winning entry "Ole Miss." Meek's source term unknown; some historians theorize made diminutive "old Mississippi" derived term from "ol' missus," term plantation's "old mistress." This sobriquet only chosen yearbook, also became name which university informally known. "Ole Miss" defined school's intangible spirit, which separate from tangible aspects university.
university began medical education 1903, when University Mississippi School Medicine established Oxford campus. that era, university provided two-year pre-clinical education certificates, graduates went state complete doctor medicine degrees. 1950, Mississippi Legislature voted create four-year medical school. July 1955, University Medical Center opened capital Jackson, Mississippi, four-year medical school. University Mississippi Medical Center, called, health sciences campus University Mississippi. houses University Mississippi School Medicine along with five other health science schools: nursing, dentistry, professions, graduate studies pharmacy (The School Pharmacy split between Oxford University Mississippi Medical Center campuses).
Several attempts were made executive legislative branches Mississippi state government relocate otherwise close University Mississippi. Mississippi Legislature between 1900 1930 introduced several bills aiming accomplish this, such legislation ever passed either house. such bill introduced 1912 Senator William Ellis Carthage, Mississippi, which would have merged college with A&M. However, this measure soundly defeated, despite bill only seeking form exploratory committee. February 1920, members legislature arrived campus discussed with students faculty idea consolidating A&M, College Women Miss located Jackson, rather than appropriate $750,000.00 funds requested Joseph Powers which were needed repair dilapidated structurally unsound buildings campus, which discovered following partial collapse dormitory 1917 scathing review other buildings later that same year state architect. These funds, plus additional $300,000.00 were appropriated school, which used build male dormitories, female dormitory pharmacy building, which partially resolved longstanding issue inadequate dormitory space students. During 1930s, Mississippi Governor Theodore Bilbo, populist, tried move university Jackson. Chancellor Alfred Hume gave state legislators grand tour Miss surrounding historic city Oxford, persuading them keep original setting.
During World colleges universities nationally that took part V-12 Navy College Training Program, which offered students path Navy commission.
Desegregation came Miss early 1960s with activities United States Force veteran James Meredith from Kosciusko, Mississippi. Even Meredith's initial efforts required great courage. involved knew violently William David McCain white political establishment Mississippi recently reacted similar efforts Clyde Kennard enroll Mississippi Southern College (now University Southern Mississippi).
Meredith lawsuit that allowed admission University Mississippi September 1962. attempted enter campus September September again September only blocked Mississippi Governor Ross Barnett, proclaimed that "...No school state will integrated while your Governor. shall everything power prevent integration schools."
After United States Court Appeals Fifth Circuit held both Barnett Lieutenant Governor Paul Johnson, contempt, with fines more than $10,000 each they refused allow Meredith enroll, Meredith, escorted force U.S. Marshals, entered campus September 1962.
people were killed gunfire during riot, French journalist, Paul Guihard Oxford repairman, Gunter. One-third Marshals, men, were injured, were soldiers National Guardsmen.
After control federal forces, Meredith, thanks protection afforded federal marshals, able enroll attend first class October Following riot, elements Army National Guard division were stationed Oxford prevent future similar violence. While most Miss students riot prior official enrollment university, many harassed Meredith during first semesters campus.
According first-person accounts, students living Meredith's dorm bounced basketballs floor just above room through hours night. When Meredith walked into cafeteria meals, students eating would turn their backs. Meredith table with other students, whom were white, they would immediately move another table. Many these events featured 2012 ESPN documentary film Ghosts Miss.

university adopted on-field mascot athletic events fall 2010. Colonel Reb, retired from sidelines sporting events 2003, officially replaced "Rebel", black bear, then, 2018, replaced Landshark. university sports teams still officially referred Rebels.
university's 25th Rhodes Scholar named 2008. Since 1998, produced Rhodes Scholars, well Goldwater Scholars, seven Truman Scholars, Fulbright Scholars, Marshall Scholar, three Udall Scholars, Gates Cambridge Scholars, Mitchell Scholar, Boren Scholars, Boren fellow German Chancellor Fellowship.
ratio University Mississippi 19:1, school 47.4 percent classes with fewer than students. most popular majors University Mississippi include: Integrated Marketing Elementary Education Teaching; Management, General; Accountancy, Finance, General; Pharmacy, Sciences, Other; Biology, Psychology Criminal Justice; Business Management, General. average freshman retention rate, indicator student success satisfaction, 85.3 percent.

University Mississippi Field Station Abbeville natural laboratory used study, research teach about sustainable freshwater ecosystems.

Croft students combine regional concentration Europe, East Asia, Latin America, Middle East with thematic concentration global economics business, international governance politics, social cultural identity. program emphasizes intensive foreign language training, qualitative quantitative skills, mandatory study abroad semester more, yearlong senior thesis.

University Mississippi's main campus located Oxford. There also regional campuses Booneville, DeSoto, Grenada, Tupelo.
University Mississippi Oxford original campus, beginning with only square-mile land. main campus today contains around 1,200 acres land. Also, University Mississippi owns golf course airport Oxford. golf course airport considered part University Mississippi, Oxford campus well.
buildings main University Mississippi campus come from Georgian architecture; however, some newer buildings today have more contemporary architecture. first building built Oxford campus Lyceum, only original building remaining. construction Lyceum began 1846 completed 1848. Where Farley Hall located, prior building referred "Dead House" where bodies deceased soldiers were stored.

Today University Mississippi campus, most buildings have been completely renovated newly constructed. There currently least residential buildings Oxford campus, with more being built. Daily Mississippian (DM) newspaper university, established 1911. Although located Miss campus, operated largely independent newspaper students. only college newspaper state that published five times week. staff consists approximately editors, about writers photographers, five-person student sales staff. Daily circulation 12,000. award-winning publication celebrated 100th anniversary 2011–12.
online version Daily Mississippian also includes original content that supplements print publication photo galleries, videos, breaking news student blogs. Page views average 360,000 month.

Approximately 5,300 students live campus residence halls, residential colleges apartment complexes. freshmen (students with less than credit hours) required live campus housing their first year unless they meet certain commuter guidelines. Department Student Housing auxiliary, meaning that does receive from state funds. rent received from students pays housing functions such utilities, staff salaries, furniture, supplies, repairs, renovations buildings. Most residence staff members students, including day-to-day management, conduct board members maintenance personnel. Upon acceptance University Mississippi, housing application submitted with application fee. cost on-campus housing ranges from approximately $4,000 more than $8,000 (the highest price being that newly renovated Village apartments) academic calendar year, depending occupancy room type. Students (with more than credit hours) have option live campus unaffiliated housing.
Graduate students, undergraduate students aged older, students married, students with families live Village Apartments. complex consists story buildings, adjacent University Mississippi School. over married students, graduate students live one-bedroom apartments. Graduate students students over live studio style apartments. Students with children live two-bedroom apartments. Children living Village Apartments zoned Oxford School District. Residents zoned Bramlett Elementary School (PreK-1), Oxford Elementary School (2-3), Della Davidson Elementary School (4-5), Oxford Middle School (6-8), Oxford High School (9-12).

Delta Kappa Epsilon followed shortly after Miss 1850, first have house campus Mississippi. Delta Gamma Women's Fraternity founded 1873 Lewis School Girls nearby Oxford. Greek life Miss suspended from 1912 1926 statewide legislation.
Today, sorority chapters very large, with some boasting over active members. Recruitment fiercely competitive potential sorority members encouraged secure personal from Miss sorority alumnae increase chances receiving invitation join eleven sororities campus. Fraternity recruitment also fierce, with only active chapters campus.
Associated Student Body (ASB) Miss student government organization. Students elected Senate spring semester, with leftover seats voted fall. Senators represent groups organizations such Greek councils sports clubs, they represent their school (i.e., College Liberal Arts School Engineering). officers also elected spring semester, following weeks campaigning. common tradition among Miss students that, after they vote elections, they local convenience stores purchase Coca-Cola PepsiCo brand soft drinks. Following elections, newly sworn-in officers release applications, conduct interviews, eventually choose their cabinet members coming school year. student body, excluding Medical Center, includes 18,121 2,089 graduate students, students students Doctor Pharmacy program. comprise 12.8 percent student body.
